commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a macOS launchd pre-write guard that refuses to install or stage a user LaunchAgent when a same-label system LaunchDaemon is loaded or present on disk, with focused launchd regression tests.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +75. Total +123 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level with live reporter and contributor proof. Current main writes and bootstraps only the gui-domain LaunchAgent, while the linked issue and PR body show the system LaunchDaemon precondition and before/after behavior.
commands.review_followup: - Maintainer review should decide whether to accept the intentional fail-closed behavior and whether this narrow PR or the broader sibling PR should be the landing path; no automated repair is indicated.

target.title: fix(daemon): refuse duplicate launchd gateway managers
target.body:
Closes #97178

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators running the gateway under a system-domain LaunchDaemon could later run `openclaw gateway install` from a user session and silently add a duplicate gui-domain LaunchAgent for the same gateway label and port. That duplicate manager setup can restart-loop the gateway and disconnect clients.

## Why This Change Was Made

The macOS launchd installer now checks for a same-label system LaunchDaemon before it writes or bootstraps the user LaunchAgent. If `launchctl print system/<label>` succeeds or a matching `/Library/LaunchDaemons/<label>.plist` exists, install refuses with recovery guidance instead of creating a second KeepAlive manager. This intentionally stays narrow and does not add full system LaunchDaemon install/stop/uninstall lifecycle support.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the change: it adds a pre-write launchd conflict guard and focused mocked launchd regressions for the reported duplicate-manager path.

## User Impact

Headless macOS operators who already manage the gateway as a system LaunchDaemon get a clear stop-and-fix error instead of a second user LaunchAgent that can fight the system daemon on the same port.

## Evidence

Behavior addressed: `openclaw gateway install` refuses to write/bootstrap a gui-domain LaunchAgent when a same-label system LaunchDaemon is loaded or present on disk.

Real environment tested: local source checkout with mocked launchd states; the issue reporter supplied live headless macOS LaunchDaemon evidence on #97178. I did not mutate a real system LaunchDaemon on this host.

Exact steps:

```shell
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts src/daemon/inspect.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: the new launchd tests cover both `launchctl print system/ai.openclaw.gateway` reporting a loaded daemon and `/Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist` existing when the system job is not printed.

Observed result: focused launchd test passed; the broader daemon/status proof set passed in 2 Vitest shards.

What was not tested: live install against a disposable headless macOS host with a real system LaunchDaemon, to avoid creating or disturbing host-level launchd services from this automation run.


---

**Runtime proof — captured before/after (negative-control harness, macOS, Node 22):**

Exercised end-to-end through the real `installLaunchAgent` → `writeLaunchAgentPlist` → `assertNoSystemLaunchDaemonConflict` → real `execFile` path; only the external `launchctl` binary is mocked (a PATH shim), plist writes go to a scratch `HOME`. Same harness ran on unfixed `main` and on this branch.

_Before — unfixed `main` (negative control):_

```
Scenario A (no conflict, proceeds)  : PASS
Scenario B (conflict, refuses)      : FAIL
>>> UNFIXED: with a same-label system LaunchDaemon present, install did NOT
    refuse and WROTE the gui LaunchAgent plist anyway.  (the #97178 bug)
```

_After — this branch:_

```
Scenario A (no conflict, proceeds)  : PASS
Scenario B (conflict, refuses)      : PASS
error (verbatim): Existing system LaunchDaemon system/ai.openclaw.gateway detected at
  /Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist. Refusing to install a gui-domain
  LaunchAgent for the same gateway label because duplicate launchd managers can
  restart-loop the gateway. ... sudo launchctl bootout system/ai.openclaw.gateway ...
plist written : false
>>> FIXED: the guard refused the duplicate manager with recovery guidance.
```

**What was not tested:** a live install against a disposable macOS host with a real system LaunchDaemon over a live port; and the on-disk `/Library/LaunchDaemons/<label>.plist` branch (needs a root-owned path via `sudo touch`/`rm`) — it shares the same throw site as the proven `launchctl print` branch.


---

**Branch-(b) proof — REAL root-owned `/Library/LaunchDaemons` plist (addresses the "mock-backed proof" review note):**

Re-ran on macOS with a genuine root-owned `/Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist` (`sudo touch`), and with `launchctl print system/<label>` forced to report NOT-loaded (exit 113) so the **only** conflict signal is the real on-disk system plist — the `launchctl` shim plays no part in the decision. This exercises the real `/Library/LaunchDaemons` filesystem check (`LAUNCH_DAEMON_SYSTEM_DIR`) end-to-end.

_PR branch (`9f0f82df78`) — exit 0:_

```
launchctl print system/<label> : NOT-loaded (113)  -> branch-(a) inert
real /Library/LaunchDaemons plist present : yes     -> branch-(b) is the signal
install threw  : true
error (verbatim):
  | Existing system LaunchDaemon system/ai.openclaw.gateway detected at /Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist.
  | Refusing to install a gui-domain LaunchAgent for the same gateway label because duplicate launchd managers can restart-loop the gateway.
  | Keep the system LaunchDaemon, or remove it first with `sudo launchctl bootout system/ai.openclaw.gateway` and `sudo rm /Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`, then rerun `openclaw gateway install`.
gui LaunchAgent written : false
>>> FIXED: install refused via the REAL root-owned system plist (branch-b).
```

_`main` (`29f5e9d35c`, no guard) — exit 1, negative control:_

```
launchctl print system/<label> : NOT-loaded (113)   real plist present : yes
install threw  : false
gui LaunchAgent written : true
>>> UNFIXED: install IGNORED the real system plist and wrote the gui LaunchAgent anyway — the #97178 bug.
```
